heading: Assistance for Cuban people
label: 6062 Assistance for Cuban people

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President shall develop a plan for providing economic assistance to Cuba at such time as the President determines that a transition government or a democratically elected government in Cuba (as determined under ) is in power.section 6063(c) of this title

children.heading: Effect on other laws
children.label: 2 Effect on other laws
children.text: Assistance may be provided under this section subject to an authorization of appropriations and subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.label: A
children.text: to Cuba when a transition government in Cuba is in power; and

children.label: B
children.text: to Cuba when a democratically elected government in Cuba is in power.

children.label: I
children.text: such food, medicine, medical supplies and equipment, and assistance to meet emergency energy needs, as is necessary to meet the basic human needs of the Cuban people; and

children.label: II
children.text: assistance described in subparagraph (C).

children.label: ii
children.text: Assistance in addition to assistance under clause (i) may be provided, but only after the President certifies to the appropriate congressional committees, in accordance with procedures applicable to reprogramming notifications under section 634A of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 [], that such assistance is essential to the successful completion of the transition to democracy.22 U.S.C. 2394–1

children.label: iii
children.text: Only after a transition government in Cuba is in power, freedom of individuals to travel to visit their relatives without any restrictions shall be permitted.

children.label: i
children.text: assistance under chapter 1 of part I [ et seq.] (relating to development assistance), and chapter 4 of part II [ et seq.] (relating to the economic support fund), of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961;22 U.S.C. 215122 U.S.C. 2346

children.label: ii
children.text: assistance under the Food for Peace Act [ et seq.];7 U.S.C. 1691

children.label: iii
children.text: financing, guarantees, and other forms of assistance provided by the Export-Import Bank of the United States;

children.label: iv
children.text: financial support provided by the United States International Development Finance Corporation for investment projects in Cuba;

children.label: v
children.text: assistance provided by the Trade and Development Agency;

children.label: vi
children.text: Peace Corps programs; and

children.label: vii
children.text: other appropriate assistance to carry out the policy of .section 6061 of this title

children.heading: Military adjustment assistance
children.label: C Military adjustment assistance
children.text: Assistance to a transition government in Cuba and to a democratically elected government in Cuba shall also include assistance in preparing the Cuban military forces to adjust to an appropriate role in a democracy.

children.heading: Strategy for distribution
children.label: c Strategy for distribution
children.text: The plan developed under subsection (b) shall include a strategy for distributing assistance under the plan.

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: d Distribution
children.text: Assistance under the plan developed under subsection (b) shall be provided through United States Government organizations and nongovernmental organizations and private and voluntary organizations, whether within or outside the United States, including humanitarian, educational, labor, and private sector organizations.

to seek to obtain the agreement of other countries and of international financial institutions and multilateral organizations to provide to a transition government in Cuba, and to a democratically elected government in Cuba, assistance comparable to that provided by the United States under this chapter; and
to work with such countries, institutions, and organizations to coordinate all such assistance programs.

children.heading: Communication with Cuban people
children.label: f Communication with Cuban people
children.text: The President shall take the necessary steps to communicate to the Cuban people the plan for assistance developed under this section.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: g Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the President shall transmit to the appropriate congressional committees a report describing in detail the plan developed under this section.March 12, 1996

children.heading: Report on trade and investment relations
children.label: h Report on trade and investment relations
A
children.text: acts, policies, and practices which constitute significant barriers to, or distortions of, United States trade in goods or services or foreign direct investment with respect to Cuba;

children.label: i
children.text: reciprocal extension of nondiscriminatory trade treatment (most-favored-nation treatment);

children.label: ii
children.text: designation of Cuba as a beneficiary developing country under title V of the Trade Act of 1974 [ et seq.] (relating to the Generalized System of Preferences) or as a beneficiary country under the Caribbean Basin Economic Recovery Act [ et seq.], and the implications of such designation with respect to trade with any other country that is such a beneficiary developing country or beneficiary country or is a party to the North American Free Trade Agreement; and19 U.S.C. 246119 U.S.C. 2701

children.label: iii
children.text: negotiations regarding free trade, including the accession of Cuba to the North American Free Trade Agreement;

children.label: C
children.text: specific trade negotiating objectives of the United States with respect to Cuba, including the objectives described in section 108(b)(5) of the North American Free Trade Agreement Implementation Act (); and19 U.S.C. 3317(b)(5)1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: D
children.text: actions proposed or anticipated to be undertaken, and any proposed legislation necessary or appropriate, to achieve any of such policy and negotiating objectives.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: The President shall consult with the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate and the appropriate congressional committees and shall seek advice from the appropriate advisory committees established under section 135 of the Trade Act of 1974 [] regarding the policy and negotiating objectives and the legislative proposals described in paragraph (1).19 U.S.C. 2155
